standardization_map.standard_term: Huffman Code Assignment (0 for left, 1 for right)

standardization_map.colloquial: पात का मेंक भी आए भान जीरो रीक

teaching_sequence: Huffman Code Assignment (0 for left, 1 for right)
Number of bits required for a character

concepts.name: Binary Representation
concepts.description: The method of representing data using only two symbols, typically 0 and 1.

concepts.name: Number of Bits Used
concepts.description: The count of binary digits (bits) required to represent a specific value or range of values.

concepts.name: Fixed-Length Encoding
concepts.description: A coding scheme where every character or symbol is represented by a binary code of the same predefined length.

concepts.name: Characters / Alphabet
concepts.description: The set of individual symbols or letters that constitute the data to be encoded.

concepts.name: Frequency of Characters
concepts.description: The count of how many times each character appears within a given piece of text or data.

concepts.name: Minimum Frequency
concepts.description: Identifying the character or node that has the lowest count of occurrences.

concepts.name: Selecting Minimum Frequency Nodes
concepts.description: The process of choosing the two nodes (characters or sub-trees) with the smallest frequencies for the next step in Huffman tree construction.

concepts.name: Merge Nodes
concepts.description: Combining two selected nodes to form a new parent node in a hierarchical tree structure.

concepts.name: Merged Node Frequency Calculation
concepts.description: Summing the frequencies of the two merged child nodes to determine the frequency of their newly created parent node.

concepts.name: Huffman Tree Construction
concepts.description: The iterative process of building a binary tree by repeatedly merging the two nodes with the lowest frequencies until a single root node remains.

concepts.name: Root Node
concepts.description: The single topmost node in a Huffman tree, which represents the total frequency of all characters.

concepts.name: Leaf Nodes
concepts.description: The terminal nodes in a Huffman tree, each representing an individual character from the alphabet.

concepts.name: Huffman Code Assignment (0 for left, 1 for right)
concepts.description: The technique of assigning binary digits (0 to left branches and 1 to right branches) while traversing down the Huffman tree to form codewords.

concepts.name: Path to Leaf Node
concepts.description: The unique sequence of 0s and 1s encountered when traversing from the root node to a specific leaf node.

concepts.name: Code Words
concepts.description: The variable-length binary strings that uniquely represent each character, derived from its path in the Huffman tree.

concepts.name: Number of bits required for a character
concepts.description: The length of the specific Huffman codeword assigned to a character, which varies based on its frequency.

concepts.name: Total bits for message
concepts.description: The sum of (frequency of each character multiplied by its codeword length), representing the total size of the compressed message.

concepts.name: Data Compression
concepts.description: The process of reducing the number of bits needed to represent data, typically by encoding frequent data items with shorter codes.

concepts.name: Message Size Calculation (Bytes)
concepts.description: Converting the total number of bits in a message (compressed or uncompressed) into its equivalent size in bytes.

prerequisites.rationale: Understanding binary representation is fundamental to comprehend how bits are used.
The concept of a fixed number of bits per item is the basis for fixed-length encoding.
Characters are the entities for which frequencies are calculated.
Frequency data is required to identify the minimum frequency.
Identifying minimum frequency characters is the first step in selecting nodes for merging.
Nodes must be selected before they can be merged.
After merging nodes, their combined frequency must be calculated.
Repeated merging and frequency calculation of nodes leads to the construction of the Huffman Tree.
The Root Node is an inherent component formed during Huffman Tree Construction.
Leaf Nodes are the characters placed at the terminal points of the Huffman Tree during its construction.
The structure of the Huffman Tree is essential for assigning binary codes to its branches.
The assignment of 0s and 1s to branches defines the path to any leaf node.
The sequence of bits along the path to a leaf node forms its unique codeword.
The length of a codeword directly determines the number of bits required for that character.
Character frequencies are needed, along with codeword lengths, to calculate the total bits for the message.
The variable number of bits per character is used to calculate the total compressed message size.
Data compression (Huffman coding) is often explained by contrasting its efficiency against fixed-length encoding.
The total number of bits must be known to convert the message size into bytes.
